model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Illinois holds a commanding ~11-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over North Carolina (KenPom #7, AdjEM +32.14 vs. KenPom #29, AdjEM +20.86), anchored by the nation's #1 adjusted offense (AdjOE 131.9 by Torvik) that is perfectly positioned to exploit a UNC defense ranked only 34th nationally. The Tar Heels are severely undermined by the confirmed season-ending broken thumb suffered by All-ACC freshman Caleb Wilson — their best player — who is out for the entire tournament, a personnel loss that already suppressed UNC's seeding and explains why they opened as only a 2.5-point favorite over VCU in the first round. Illinois's Big Ten battle-testing, superior two-way efficiency on both ends of the floor, and no significant injury concerns give them a clear and decisive edge over a UNC team operating well below its pre-injury ceiling. The combination of Illinois's elite offensive firepower, the Wilson-shaped hole in UNC's roster, and the consistent efficiency gap across all major rating systems make the Illini a confident pick to advance to the Sweet 16.

reasoningStep.title: Illinois vs. North Carolina — Round of 32 South Region Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Illinois is a dominant favorite backed by the nation's #1 offense, a top-24 defense, and a ~11-point KenPom efficiency gap over a North Carolina team missing its best player in Caleb Wilson. All major metrics, matchup dynamics, and health context point decisively to Illinois advancing.
evidence: Illinois: KenPom #7 (AdjEM +32.14), Torvik #6 (AdjOE 131.9 — #2 nationally, AdjDE 98.1 — #24 nationally). North Carolina: KenPom #29 (AdjEM +20.86), Torvik #27 (AdjOE 121.5 — #32 nationally, AdjDE 100.0 — #34 nationally). Efficiency gap: ~11.3 KenPom points.

Caleb Wilson (UNC All-ACC freshman, leading scorer) confirmed out for the entire 2026 NCAA Tournament with a season-ending broken thumb, a loss that already impacted UNC's seeding and caps their ceiling heading into single-elimination play.

Illinois's #1 nationally ranked offense (AdjOE 131.9) faces UNC's defense ranked 34th nationally — a favorable matchup. UNC's offense ranked 33rd faces Illinois's defense ranked 24th — also favoring Illinois. Both ends of the floor advantage go to the Illini.

No significant Illinois injury concerns surfaced across multiple searches, while UNC's first-round pick over VCU carried only 0.54 confidence reflecting how thin their margin was even against an 11-seed without Wilson.

Illinois is the #3 seed in the South region with a 24-8 record forged against elite Big Ten competition, a level of schedule-strength battle-testing that UNC's ACC résumé — already diminished by Wilson's absence — cannot match in this matchup.

Illinois's Big Ten battle-testing, superior two-way efficiency on both ends of the floor, and no significant injury concerns give them a clear and decisive edge over a UNC team operating well below its pre-injury ceiling.
